name: Snowflake
description: Cloud data platform providing data warehouse, data lake, and data application capabilities; exposes a REST SQL API plus SDKs and drivers to run SQL and manage objects programmatically.

auth_methods.note: Snowflake SQL API docs list supported methods as OAuth (using an OAuth access token) and key-pair authentication (sign a JWT). Requests use an Authorization: Bearer <token> header where the bearer token is either an OAuth access token or a JWT.

credential_access.note: Snowflake's official Get Started page advertises a self-serve free trial sign-up ("Start for free" / "Start your free trial"), indicating developers can create an account without sales approval to obtain access for building/testing.

api_surface.note: The SQL API docs state the SQL API is a REST API for sending SQL statements over HTTPS. Because the SQL API executes SQL statements, it can access and manage a wide range of Snowflake objects via SQL, so breadth is 'broad'. Additional SDKs and drivers are documented on the developer hub.

buildability.note: Snowflake provides public SQL API documentation including supported authentication (OAuth access token or key-pair/JWT bearer token) and offers a self-serve free trial sign-up flow, so a developer can obtain an account and start integrating immediately (with potential paid usage later depending on consumption).

queries: Snowflake developer API documentation root docs.snowflake.com API
